Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Nishiki Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Nishiki Holdings Pte Ltd (NH) is a company incorporated in Singapore and functions as a holding company.
At the time of the Sale Leaseback Transaction in 2016, NH was owned 50% by Dr Masami Yabumoto and 50% by a Medical Charitable Trust.
After the Sale Leaseback Transaction, the shareholding of NH changed so that Dr Masami Yabumoto and Ms Zhang Ran were equal shareholders.
From June 2016 to about October 2018, Mr Theodore Meegan was the sole director of Nishiki Holdings Pte Ltd.
Nishiki Holdings Pte Ltd is the ordinary shareholder of Nishiki Real Estate Pte Ltd and the sole shareholder of Nishiki International Investments Pte Ltd.

On 2016-06-27, a director's resolution for Nishiki Holdings Pte Ltd identified that its sole director, Mr Theodore Meegan, also held 51% of the shares in Brocade Investment Pte Ltd and Brocade Management Pte Ltd.
On 2016-06-28, an Investment Management Deed was executed appointing Brocade Management Pte Ltd as manager to provide discretionary investment services for the group's assets.

A loan agreement was executed between the Nishiki entities and Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L, providing for a principal loan of JPY100 million and an Additional Interest Amount calculated by reference to the fair market value of the group's Japanese assets.
The loan agreement defined an Appraiser as a reputable appraisal firm with operations in Japan appointed by the lender in consultation with the Borrower Agent.
On 2017-06-29, Nishiki Real Estate Pte Ltd repaid the JPY100 million loan principal and fixed interest in full.
Nishiki Tokutei Mokuteki Kaisha is a Japanese special purpose company wholly owned by Nishiki Real Estate Pte Ltd and holds nine medical properties in Osaka.
On 4 December 2018, Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L sent a demand for payment of an Additional Interest Amount to contacts including Mr Theodore Meegan and Mr Shinsuke Kataoka.
Ms Eve Lee of Sankaty emailed Mr Shinsuke Kataoka proposing Houlihan Lokey, PwC or Deloitte as potential appraisers for the valuation.
Sankaty entered into an engagement with Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited to act as appraiser for determining the fair market value of the Osaka properties.
Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited subcontracted the physical valuation of the nine Osaka medical properties to Enrix Co Ltd, a Japanese valuation firm.
Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L appointed joint private receivers over the plaintiffs' charged assets.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the plaintiffs were contractually bound to pay an Additional Interest Amount under the loan agreement and whether the lender's appointment of an appraiser and receivers complied with the agreed mechanisms.
Party_Details.Rule: Under contract law, the validity of interest mechanisms and the appointment of receivers are determined by express contractual terms, interpreted objectively in light of the commercial context and specified qualifications for valuers.
Party_Details.Application: The Nishiki entities entered into a loan agreement with Sankaty that included an 'Additional Interest' mechanism tied to the fair market value of nine properties in Osaka.  Following repayment of the loan principal, the lender engaged a China-based firm to value the Japanese assets and consulted a non-employee consultant rather than the designated borrower agent. These procedural steps resulted in a significant debt demand and the appointment of receivers, which the borrowers challenged as failing to meet the contractual conditions precedent.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court analysed whether the lender was on notice that the director lacked authority to bind the plaintiffs to the Additional Interest provisions and concluded that the loan agreement was valid. On the 'Appraiser' issue, the court examined whether the lender complied with the requirement to appoint a reputable appraisal firm with operations in Japan in consultation with Nishiki Real Estate Pte Ltd. It found that the Investment Management Deed did not confer authority on Brocade Management or Mr. Shinsuke Kataoka to act as the Borrower Agent for this consultation. The court held that consulting only Mr. Kataoka, who had a personal economic interest in the result, did not satisfy the contractual requirement to consult the borrower. Furthermore, the court interpreted 'operations in Japan' to mean actual appraisal operations; as Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited did not have such operations, it did not meet the contractual definition. Because the valuation was invalid, the lender failed to establish a valid debt, and the subsequent appointment of receivers was declared void.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L

Facts.Text: Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L is a private limited company incorporated in Luxembourg and forms part of the debt division of Bain Capital.
The Loan Agreement provided for an Additional Interest mechanism based on the fair market value of Nishiki Tokutei Mokuteki Kaisha's assets.
Sankaty received full repayment of the JPY100 million principal and fixed interest from the borrowers in June 2017.
Sankaty emailed a 'Demand for Payment of Additional Interest Amount' to the borrowers' representatives.
Ms Eve Lee of Sankaty wrote to Mr Kataoka suggesting the appointment of Houlihan Lokey, PWC or Deloitte as potential appraisers.
Sankaty executed an engagement letter with Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited to determine the fair market value of the group assets as of 30 November 2018.
Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited subsequently subcontracted the valuation work to Enrix Co Ltd and relied on their findings for its final report.
Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited issued a final valuation report stating the fair market value of the assets was approximately JPY29.188 billion.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Sankaty European Investments S.A.R.L satisfied the contractual conditions precedent for the appointment of an appraiser and the calculation of Additional Interest.
Party_Details.Rule: A lender seeking to enforce additional return mechanisms must comply with specified contractual procedures, including the consultation of designated borrower agents and ensuring the chosen appraiser meets professional and jurisdictional qualifications.
Party_Details.Application: Sankaty sought to quantify an 'Additional Interest' payment following the maturity of a group loan. It engaged Houlihan Lokey (China) to conduct a valuation of Japanese real estate. However, the firm lacked independent appraisal operations in Japan and the lender consulted a non-employee consultant who was not the authorized Borrower Agent. These procedural steps formed the basis of the challenge to the lender's subsequent enforcement actions.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted that the loan agreement was valid, but scrutinized whether the lender complied with the requirements for appointing an appraiser. It found that Brocade Management and Mr. Kataoka lacked actual or apparent authority to act as the Borrower Agent for consultation. The court also held that Houlihan Lokey (China) Limited did not meet the definition of 'Appraiser' because it did not have its own appraisal operations in Japan. As the resulting valuation was invalid, the JPY1.111 billion debt was not legally established. Consequently, the lender's demand and the appointment of private receivers lacked a valid contractual foundation.
